'''Reimis Smith''' (born 13 May 1997) is a New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and [[Melbourne Storm]] as a {{rlp|wg}}er in the NRL and has played for New Zealand 9s at international level.

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Sydney|Sydney, New South Wales, Australia]], and is of [[Māori people|Māori]] and [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]] descent.<ref name="autogenerated1">{{cite web|last=Rothfield|first=Phil|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/new-nrl-boss-david-smith-to-earn-at-least-12m-a-year/story-fndujljl-1226523494990|title=Catch Australia's best sporting gossip with Phil Rothfield – What's the Buzz|publisher=News.com.au|date=2012-11-25|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref> Smith is the son of former [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international [[Tyran Smith]],<ref name="autogenerated2">{{cite web|author=Michael Chammas|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/canterbury-bulldogs-coach-des-hasler-lashes-out-at-nrl-scheduling-and-nsw-blues-medical-staff-20160529-gp6mtq.html|title=Canterbury Bulldogs coach Des Hasler lashes out at NRL scheduling and NSW Blues medical staff|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref> and the nephew of boxer and former professional [[rugby league]] footballer [[Anthony Mundine]].<ref name="autogenerated1"/>

Smith played his junior rugby league for the [[Mascot Juniors|Mascot Jets]] and for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] Harold Matthews team. He was educated at [[Matraville Sports High School]] and then being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2013/05/20/three-south-sydney-players-selected-in-2013-nsw-under-16s-team/|title=Three South Sydney Players Selected in 2013 NSW Under 16s Team|website=www.rabbitohs.com.au}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2015, Smith played for the [[Parramatta Eels]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131072555/http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=31 January 2016|title=S|publisher=Nyc Database|date=|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref> before joining the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] mid-season.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2015/05/12/round_10_nrl_team_an.html|title=Round 10 NRL Team Announcement|publisher=Bulldogs|date=|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref>

===2016 – 2020: Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs===
[[File:Reimissmith.jpg|thumb|Smith playing for the Bulldogs in 2016]]
On 7 May, Smith played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/95512/default.aspx|title=Updated: Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|accessdate=2016-05-24|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160604182259/http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/95512/default.aspx|archivedate=4 June 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> In Round 12 of the [[2016 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Canterbury against [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]],<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2016/05/29/nrl_late_mail_round_.af_news.html|title=NRL Late Mail: Round 12 v Raiders|publisher=Bulldogs|date=|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-raiders-v-bulldogs/tabid/10874/newsid/96435/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Raiders v Bulldogs|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref> scoring two tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/raiders-romp-home-to-tame-bulldogs/tabid/10874/newsid/96449/default.aspx|title=Raiders romp home to tame Bulldogs|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|accessdate=2016-05-30}}</ref> He had played for the clubs [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] side the day before, but due to regular Canterbury centre [[Josh Morris (rugby league)|Josh Morris]] being called up to the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] side on the day of the NRL game, Smith was called up and had to drive from Sydney to [[Canberra]] to make the game.<ref name="autogenerated2"/>

Smith spent 2017 playing for Canterbury's NSW Cup team, making no appearances for the first-grade team. On 6 May, Smith again played at centre for the Junior Kiwis against Junior Kangaroos in the 46–22 loss at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/05/05/junior-roos-power-past-kiwi-counterparts/|title=Junior Roos power past Kiwi counterparts|date=5 May 2017|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

in 2018, following the mid-season departure of [[Moses Mbye]] to the [[Wests Tigers]], Smith earned a recall back into Canterbury's first-grade team on the wing as [[Will Hopoate]] shifted from centre to fullback to fill in the void. He made his first appearance of the season in Round 15 against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], playing on the wing.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/jun/16/goodbye-to-mbye-as-bulldogs-slump-to-worst-start-in-54-years|title=Goodbye to Mbye as Bulldogs slump to worst start in 54 years|agency=Australian Associated Press|date=16 June 2018|website=the Guardian}}</ref> In Round 24 against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], Smith scored a hat-trick of tries in Canterbury's shock upset 38–0 win at [[Jubilee Oval]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/08/26/reimis-smith-stars-as-canterbury-bulldogs-stun-st-george-illawarra-dragons/|title=Reimis runs amok as Bulldogs demolish Dragons|date=26 August 2018|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> He finished the [[2018 NRL season|season]] with 11 matches and 7 tries. On 3 September, he was selected in the [[New Zealand Kiwis]] training squad but didn’t make the final 24-man squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/03/canterbury-bulldogs-rookie-reimis-smith-joins-returning-stars-in-extended-new-zealand-kiwis-squad/|title=Bulldogs rookie Smith in Kiwis Test squad|date=3 September 2018|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> On 3 October, Smith extended his contract with Canterbury to the end of the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/03/reimis-smith-re-signs-with-canterbury-bulldogs-until-end-of-2020/|title=Reimis re-signs with Canterbury until end of 2020|date=3 October 2018|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

In Round 8  of the [[2019 NRL season]] against Manly-Warringah, Smith scored two tries in a 18–10 defeat at [[Brookvale Oval]].  In Round 19, Smith scored two tries in a 20–12 loss against the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/hasler-completes-homecoming-with-win-against-bulldogs-20190504-p51k3k.html/|title=Hasler completes homecoming with win against Bulldogs|website=SMH}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-07-28/nrl-scorecentre-buldogs-roosters-panthers-raiders/11347882/|title=Raiders and Sydney Roosters consolidate spots in NRL top four with round 19 wins|website=ABC}}</ref>

In Round 23 against arch rivals Parramatta, Smith was placed on report and then sent to the sin bin in the second half of the match after headbutting Parramatta player [[Maika Sivo]] during Canterbury's upset 12–6 victory at the [[Western Sydney Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/heads-gone-reimis-smith-faces-possible-ban-after-aiming-headbutt-at-parramatta-eels-sivo/news-story/09858318a811fe0cfb929d6c79732f8d/|title=Reimis Smith faces possible ban after aiming headbutt at Parramatta Eels Sivo|website=Fox Sports}}</ref>

On 11 November, Smith signed a contract extension to stay at Canterbury until the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/11/11/reimis-re-signs-with-canterbury-until-end-of-2021/|title=Reimis re-signs with Canterbury until end of 2021|website=NRL}}</ref>

Smith played 14 games for Canterbury in the [[2020 NRL season]] scoring four tries.  The club finished in 15th place on the table, only avoiding the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]] by for and against over bottom side Brisbane. On 13 November, Smith was granted a release by Canterbury to join reigning premiers, [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/reimis-smith-canterbury-bulldogs-nrl-2020-release-contract-melbourne-storm-rugby-league/1myranymyr5uc17dyjejyg95eb|title=Reimis Smith signs with Melbourne Storm on two-year deal following Canterbury Bulldogs release|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===2021 – 2024: Melbourne Storm===
Smith played a total of 25 games for Melbourne in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club won 19 matches in a row and claimed the [[Minor Premiership]].  Smith played in two finals matches including the preliminary final where Melbourne suffered a shock 10–6 loss against eventual premiers [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-25/penrith-panthers-beat-melbourne-storm-to-reach-nrl-grand-final/100485860/|title=Penrith Panthers beat Melbourne Storm 10–6 to reach second straight NRL grand final, will face South Sydney Rabbitohs|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>

In round 9 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Smith was taken from the field during Melbourne's victory over [[St. George Illawarra]].  It was later announced Smith would miss 10–12 weeks with a torn pectoral injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/storm-coach-craig-bellamy-reveals-full-extent-ryan-papenhuyzen-reimis-smith-injuries/qi841f1ofb6etmtjhpxzaana/|title=Storm coach Craig Bellamy reveals full extent of Ryan Papenhuyzen, Reimis Smith injuries|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

Smith made 22 appearances for Melbourne in the [[2023 NRL season]] including the club's preliminary final loss against Penrith.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-melbourne-storm-season-review/af9dab1e4f900cda437dc53f#:~:text=The%20expectations%20for%20the%20Storm,clubs%2C%20but%20not%20the%20Storm./|title=NRL 2023: Melbourne Storm season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref> He would score five tries for the season, forming a partnership on the right edge with rookie winger [[Will Warbrick]]. Smith also made three appearances for the [[Brisbane Tigers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] during the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Season in Review: Reimis Smith |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2023/10/24/season-in-review-reimis-smith/ |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=24 October 2023 |date=24 October 2023}}</ref>

On 31 July 2024, it was announced that Smith had been granted an immediate release from his contract to take up an opportunity elsewhere.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-07-31 |title=NRL contract news: Melbourne Storm grant Reimis Smith immediate release {{!}} Sporting News Australia |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-contract-news-melbourne-storm-reimis-smith-release/789416fcd878560a97d4654d |access-date=2024-07-31 |website=www.sportingnews.com |language=en-au}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Pryde |first=Scott |date=2024-07-31 |title=Storm make surprise call on experienced centre's immediate future |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/storm-make-surprise-call-on-experienced-centres-immediate-future-212968/ |access-date=2024-07-31 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>

===2024 – 2025: Catalans Dragons===
On 1 August 2024, it was reported that he had joined [[Super League]] club [[Catalans Dragons]] for the remainder of the season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League |date=1 Aug 2024|title= Melbourne Storm centre’s Super League move confirmed as Reimis Smith’s new club identified|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/melbourne-storm-centres-super-league-move-confirmed-as-reimis-smiths-new-club-identified}}</ref> On 1 September 2025, Smith announced he would be retiring from the sport at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2025-09-01 |title=Former Bulldogs, Storm centre announces retirement |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/former-bulldogs-storm-centre-announces-retirement-226165/ |access-date=2025-09-01 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>
